title: buildah loses a tagged container
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

Buildah removes the parent image tag when basing a 2nd container on it.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. podman images -a
```
REPOSITORY  TAG     IMAGE ID  CREATED  SIZE
```
2. cat Dockerfile.compile 
```Dockerfile
FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi

RUN echo "Do a long compile here" | tee /test.txt
```
3. buildah bud -t long-compile:latest -f Dockerfile.compile
```
STEP 1: FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 55eda7743468 done  
Copying blob 4b21dcdd136d done  
Copying config 613e5da7a9 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: RUN echo "this is a long compilation here"
this is a long compilation here
STEP 3: COMMIT long-compile:latest
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 476579af086a skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 1e8cd6732429 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 015cff8024ed done  
Copying config 1c0f06050f done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 1c0f06050f3
1c0f06050f385dfc792b3e0e092ccf1844a95479603c0d007eb75d3c7f9521a0
```
4. podman images -a
```
REPOSITORY                           TAG     IMAGE ID      CREATED        SIZE
localhost/long-compile               latest  1c0f06050f38  9 seconds ago  213 MB
registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi  latest  613e5da7a934  3 weeks ago    213 MB
```
5. cat Dockerfile
```Dockerfile
FROM long-compile:latest AS work

FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal

COPY --from=work /usr/local/ /usr/local/
```
6. buildah bud -t base:latest -f Dockerfile
```
STEP 1: FROM long-compile:latest AS work
--> 1c0f06050f3
STEP 2: FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 65c0f2178ac8 done  
Copying blob 8f403cb21126 done  
Copying config 332744c185 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 3: COPY --from=work /usr/local/ /usr/local/
STEP 4: COMMIT base:latest
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 4a2bc86056a8 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 144a43b910e8 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 5f70bf18a086 done  
Copying config a0cd06526f done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> a0cd06526f8
a0cd06526f81a675bf7576b30420d0b2a37f5d1d215b30575759c0acb38ef541
```
7. podman images -a
```
REPOSITORY                                   TAG     IMAGE ID      CREATED        SIZE
localhost/base                               latest  a0cd06526f81  9 seconds ago  105 MB
registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-minimal  latest  332744c1854d  3 weeks ago    105 MB
registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi          latest  613e5da7a934  3 weeks ago    213 MB
```

**Describe the results you received:**

the `long-compile` container was removed from podman's local repository.

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected `long-compile` to be retained for other builds.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
Listing... Done
buildah/hirsute,now 1.19.6+dfsg1-1 amd64 [installed,automatic]
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.19.6
Go Version:      go1.16
Image Spec:      1.0.1
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.10.3
Git Commit:      
Built:           Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.16
Built:        Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="21.04 (Hirsute Hippo)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 21.04"
VERSION_ID="21.04"
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
VERSION_CODENAME=hirsute
UBUNTU_CODENAME=hirsute
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux omnixps 5.11.0-16-generic #17-Ubuntu SMP Wed Apr 14 20:12:43 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
<file not found>
```

comments.body: Yes this definitely looks wrong.  @nalind PTAL
Yeah, I think I see the bug.
Hmm, I think this was fixed in time for 1.20.0 as #3025, but hasn't been backported to the 1.19 release branch.
That looks like the right symptoms.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since this is fixed in the main branch, I am going to close.

Just frustrated its not getting backported.  This is probably the biggest frustration I have with redhat products, they withhold features and bugfixes that don't have breaking changes from stable versions.  If there were breaking changes in 1.20 or 1.21, then I would understand withholding them from release to stable linux versions, but when i'm on RHEL 8, and I have to wait years to get bug fixes like this, its kinda disheartening.
